none: Environmental Impact Statement, 230 KV Transmission Line, Canadian Border to Hibbing, Minnesota

The proposal involves constructing a 230 kV transmission line from the Canadian border to Hibbing, Minnesota. This line will support the growing electricity needs of northeastern Minnesota, particularly for the expanding taconite industry. The project aims to enhance power reliability and interconnect with Manitoba Hydro, benefiting both Minnesota and North Dakota. The construction will impact several counties, involving some environmental concerns like timber cutting and soil erosion, but efforts will be made to minimize these effects.
Environmental Impact Statement, 230 KV Transmission Line, Canadian Border to Hibbing, Minnesota

The proposal involves constructing a 230 kV transmission line from the Canadian border to Hibbing, Minnesota. This line will support the growing electricity needs of northeastern Minnesota, particularly for the expanding taconite industry. The project aims to enhance power reliability and interconnect with Manitoba Hydro, benefiting both Minnesota and North Dakota. The construction will impact several counties, involving some environmental concerns like timber cutting and soil erosion, but efforts will be made to minimize these effects.

The proposed 230 kV transmission line was initiated to address the increasing electricity demands of the taconite industry in northeastern Minnesota. While there were some environmental concerns regarding timber cutting and soil erosion, the project aimed to improve power reliability and facilitate interconnection with Manitoba Hydro. Public response included discussions about environmental impacts, but the project was ultimately seen as necessary for regional economic growth.

"Without the availability of the increased electrical power, it is very possible that the anticipated expansion of the taconite industry in the region would be severely hampered." — ARDC Final Review Comments
"The proposed action will improve system reliability by enabling the transfer of power through the entire northeast Minnesota and North Dakota area over a 230 kV network." — Project Purpose Section
